purpose: To provide a functional, web-based platform enabling customers to browse products, manage shopping carts, complete mock checkout processes, place orders, and view their order history.
scope_in: Adding, updating, and removing items from a shopping cart
scope_out: Advanced inventory management (e.g., low stock alerts)
Customer account registration/login (MVP focuses on guest checkout)
business_rules: Only in-stock products can be ordered.
Cart total must be calculated dynamically based on item prices and quantities.
Payment processing is mocked for the MVP and does not interact with external financial systems.
constraints: The system must run locally (single instance deployment).
The first version focuses strictly on catalog, cart, checkout, and orders.
assumptions: Users access the system through a standard modern web browser.
Admin product management capabilities are deferred to a later phase.

functional_requirements.title: Product Catalog Browsing
functional_requirements.description: The system must allow customers to view a list of available products, supporting filtering and basic search.

acceptance_criteria.description: The system must display all active products on the main catalog page.
The system must allow filtering by category.
The system must display the product name, price, and availability status on the catalog listing.

functional_requirements.title: Product Detail Viewing
functional_requirements.description: The system must display comprehensive information for a selected product.

acceptance_criteria.description: The product detail page must display the full description, price, and SKU.
The user must be able to select a quantity and add the item to the cart from the detail page.
If a product is out of stock, the system must display a clear 'Out of Stock' message and disable the 'Add to Cart' button.

functional_requirements.title: Shopping Cart Management
functional_requirements.description: The system must allow users to manage items added to their cart.

acceptance_criteria.description: The cart must display the item name, quantity, unit price, and subtotal for every item.
Users must be able to increase or decrease the quantity of an item in the cart.
Users must be able to remove items entirely from the cart.
The cart must display a running total (subtotal) before taxes/shipping.

functional_requirements.title: Checkout Process
functional_requirements.description: The system must guide the user through the checkout steps.

acceptance_criteria.description: The checkout process must collect necessary shipping information (Name, Address, etc.).
The system must calculate and display the final total, including any mock taxes or shipping fees.
The system must provide a mock payment interface (e.g., accepting a dummy card number) to proceed.

functional_requirements.title: Order Placement
functional_requirements.description: The system must finalize the transaction and create a permanent order record.

acceptance_criteria.description: Upon successful mock payment, the system must generate a unique Order ID.
The system must confirm the order placement to the user and clear the shopping cart.

functional_requirements.title: Order History Viewing
functional_requirements.description: The system must allow the customer to view a list of their past orders.

acceptance_criteria.description: The order history page must list the Order ID, date, and total amount for each past order.
Clicking an order ID must display the detailed items, quantities, and final price breakdown for that specific order.

non_functional_requirements.description: The system must load all primary pages (Catalog, Product Detail, Cart) within 3 seconds under normal load.

acceptance_criteria.description: Page load time must be measured and maintained below 3 seconds.

non_functional_requirements.description: All user input fields (especially addresses and names) must be sanitized to prevent XSS and SQL injection attacks.

acceptance_criteria.description: The system must validate that all required fields are filled before submission.

non_functional_requirements.description: The user interface must be intuitive, requiring minimal training for a first-time user to complete a purchase.

acceptance_criteria.description: The checkout flow must be linear and clearly guide the user from one step to the next.

use_cases.title: Customer Purchases Product
preconditions: The product catalog must be populated with available items.
main_flow: Customer selects a product and views details (FR-002).
Customer provides shipping details and completes mock payment (FR-004, FR-005).
System confirms order and displays confirmation page (FR-005).
alternative_flows: Customer removes item from cart before checkout.
Customer fails mock payment and is prompted to retry.

use_cases.title: Customer Views Order History
preconditions: The customer must have placed at least one order.
main_flow: Customer navigates to Order History page (FR-006).
System displays list of past orders (FR-006).

use_cases.title: Admin Views Order Status
preconditions: The Admin must be logged into the system.
main_flow: System displays a list of recent orders.
Admin views the order details and updates the status (e.g., 'Processing' to 'Shipped').
